title: armeria bourgaei subsp. lanceobracteata
intro:
Armeria bourgaei subsp. lanceobracteata is a lesser-known species of the Armeria genus, a type of flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its white or pink flowers and compact growth habit.

This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Armeria bourgaei subsp. lanceobracteata include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional folk medicine practices.
